rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOUGLAS P. AMOS GST EXEMPT TRUST, DATED MARCH 24, 2010
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions For The Purposes Of Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ricardo Enriquez
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner California Department of State Hospitals' Motion to Consolidate Actions for the Purposes of Trial is GRANTED. Respondent Trustee Fremont Bank filed no opposition. The four actions involve common questions of law regarding liability for costs sought under Welf. & Inst. Code section 7275, and common facts. (CCP section 1048.) There is no showing that consolidation would result in undue prejudice or deprivation of substantial rights to the four trusts. (Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimants Trust (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 976, 978; Fellner v. Steinbaum (1955) 132 Cal. App. 2d 509, 511.) PTR-14-297928, PTR-14-297929, PTR-14-297930, and PTR-14-297931 are HEREBY CONSOLIDATED for trial, but without prejudice to the trial department's own decisions about how best to manage proceedings in that department. The caption of the consolidated actions shall list first the caption in PTR-14-297928 and second the captions in the following order: PTR-14-297929, PTR-14-297930, and PTR-14-297931, and indicate the actions are consolidated for the purposes of trial. The prevailing party is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on November 12, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
